claimText: India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) is a 100% government-owned entity operating under the Department of Posts, Ministry of Communications.
evidenceExcerpt: Genesis: 2018 as a 100% government-owned payments bank under the Department of Posts, Ministry of Communications.
examUse: Statement-based question on the ownership structure and nodal ministry of IPPB.
distractorHooks: Subsidiary of the Reserve Bank of India

claimText: Payment banks are permitted to accept deposits up to a maximum limit of Rs. 2 lakh.
evidenceExcerpt: It can accept deposits (up to 2 lakh)
examUse: Statement-based question on the deposit limits applicable to payment banks.

claimText: Payment banks are prohibited from giving loans or issuing credit cards.
evidenceExcerpt: cannot give loans or issue credit cards.
examUse: Statement-based question on the restricted activities of payment banks.
distractorHooks: Can issue credit cards but not loans
Can give micro-loans up to 1 lakh
Can issue both loans and credit cards

claimText: Although payment banks cannot issue loans directly, they can provide access to third-party financial products such as loans and insurance.
evidenceExcerpt: access to 3rd Party products such as loans, insurance, etc.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing between direct lending and cross-selling third-party loan products.
distractorHooks: Cannot offer any loan products, including third-party loans
Can only offer third-party insurance, not loans
